matches.match_rationale: Enrollment numbers are inherently time-ordered: historical term-over-term counts, seasonal drift, and mid-year actuals provide the signal a time-series model needs. The pain is explicitly about forecast drift (+/- 10-15%).
matches.opportunity_sketch: A forecasting model trained on 3-5 years of per-campus enrollment actuals, term-start applications, and demographic inputs produces a rolling 6-month enrollment projection with confidence bands.

matches.match_rationale: The cascading damage from enrollment drift is staffing: teachers are hired to budget headcount, then enrollment shifts, creating overages or shortfalls.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A scenario-aware staffing optimizer that takes enrollment forecast ranges and outputs optimal per-class teacher counts and support staff assignments.

matches.match_rationale: A recurring explicit pain point is that finance cannot confidently explain tuition variance to the board — communication of mid-year reforecast changes to leadership is manual and time-consuming.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An LLM-driven reporting layer that auto-generates a plain-English variance commentary whenever enrollment actuals are logged, for CFO review before board distribution.

matches.match_rationale: The serial reforecasting cycle (3-4 times per term) involves multiple steps: collecting principal headcount updates, propagating to staffing budgets, recalculating per-student cost allocations, and notifying stakeholders.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A reforecast automation agent that triggers on enrollment actuals upload, recalculates staffing and revenue budgets against current formula rules, produces updated variance reports, and routes approval requests.
